Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Tamara" or "Mosaic" or similar) to find Tamara Byrd's follow-up email (email_id: tamara_mosaic_followup_apr17, subject: "Follow-up: Prototype Damage, Expense Discrepancies & Deshawn Hartley Delivery Confirmation"). The agent needs to discover her three demands: (1) insurance status and cost liability on the damaged prototype, (2) explanation for two of Andre Washington's incorrectly approved expenses ($1,200 standing desk and $350/month coworking), and (3) written confirmation of Deshawn Hartley's Denver delivery by April 19.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "prototype" or "damage" or "incident report" or similar) to find Emeka's incident report to Tamara (email_id: mosaic_incident_report_final_001) and the original damage notification from Tamara (email_id: mosaic_damage_complaint_001). The agent needs to discover: Prototype Unit #1 ($90K value) arrived with a cracked servo motor housing and misaligned joint calibration, transported by Heartland Movers, coordinated by Blessing Okafor, who signed off on the packing remotely from Philadelphia without physically verifying the anti-vibration foam crating density spec.
Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "prototype" or "damage" or "Mosaic" or similar) to find the operations and finance channel discussions about the damage incident. The agent needs to discover: Marcus Thorne flagged a $40,000 insurance coverage gap — MoveOps' vendor liability covers a maximum of $50,000 per item, but the prototype is valued at $90,000. Hana Kim booked an insurance claim and liability accrual in QuickBooks. Emeka sent a formal incident report to Tamara within the 48-hour window.
Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: filtering by customer "Mosaic" or similar) to find the credit memo (Id: cm_mosaic_damage_claim_001) showing the $90,000 insurance claim with $50,000 vendor liability coverage and $40,000 remaining exposure pending resolution with Heartland Movers. The agent also needs to find the original Mosaic invoice (Id: inv_mosaic_april_2026_3reloc, DocNumber: INV-2026-0411) totaling $9,010 for the three April relocations.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Marcus" or "marcus.thorne" or similar) to find Marcus Thorne's email to Emeka about the stipend fraud audit findings (sent from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to emeka.diallo@moveops.com, CC dmitri.volkov@moveops.com). The agent needs to discover: Andre Washington (Mosaic) submitted an $89 receipt for a TechComfort Supply ergonomic keyboard (STIP-2026-0094) that is byte-for-byte identical (same SHA-256 hash) to a receipt submitted by Kevin Tran (Vectral). Marcus placed an administrative hold on the $89 payment. Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop) also submitted a $45 receipt from the same vendor with similar PDF metadata (STIP-2026-0097). Marcus asked Emeka to verify with Tamara Byrd (Mosaic) and Tessa Moreno (BrightLoop).
Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "auto-approved" or "standing desk" or "coworking" or similar) or search emails using search_emails (query: "expense" or "auto-categorizer" or similar) to find the ExpenseBot false approval incident on the Mosaic account. The agent needs to discover: Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk was auto-approved despite Mosaic's $800 furniture cap (a $400 overage caused by a malformed JSON config where max_amount was stored as a string instead of a number), and Andre Washington's $350/month coworking membership was auto-approved despite coworking being explicitly excluded from Mosaic's policy (the excluded_categories field was stored as a string instead of an array, causing the exclusion check to be skipped). The agent may also discover a third false approval — Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes (same excluded_categories bug), bringing the total to $885. Elena directed MoveOps to absorb cost, auto-approval paused, and human review reinstated for all expenses.
Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Deshawn" or similar) to find Deshawn Hartley's relocation record. The agent needs to discover: Tampa, FL to Denver, CO; move window April 14–19; Status: In Progress; Assigned Coordinator: Suki Patel; hard delivery deadline of April 19 because Deshawn is the lead presenter at the Mosaic Robotics booth at Automate 2026 trade show (April 21–24, Denver Convention Center), with booth setup beginning April 20.
Search CRM using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Deshawn" or similar) or crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic" or similar) to find the Deshawn Hartley CRM deal (id: deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026, amount: $1,520, dealstage: inprogress) and confirm the Automate 2026 trade show deadline context. The agent may also find the Zara Okoye damage claim deal (id: deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk) with the full incident details and insurance information in the deal description.
Look up Tamara Byrd's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Tamara" or "Byrd" or similar) to resolve the recipient to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com. The agent may also discover rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com as a CC recipient since Tamara CC'd him on the original damage complaint thread.
Send a reply to Tamara's follow-up email using reply_to_email (email_id: "tamara_mosaic_followup_apr17", sender: "emeka.diallo@moveops.com", cc: ["rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com"], content: addressing all three concerns) or send_email (sender: "emeka.diallo@moveops.com", recipients: ["tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com"], cc: ["rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com"]). Rafael Mendes (VP of Engineering, Mosaic Robotics) was CC'd on Tamara's original damage complaint and follow-up, so the reply should CC him as well. The reply must cover: (1) prototype damage update — Heartland Movers as the responsible vendor, Blessing Okafor's remote crating sign-off as the process failure, $50,000 vendor liability cap per item, $40,000 uninsured gap on the $90,000 prototype, MoveOps pursuing Heartland for recovery; (2) Andre Washington's expense discrepancies — the $1,200 standing desk exceeded Mosaic's $800 furniture cap, the $350/month coworking is excluded from Mosaic's policy, caused by a malformed ExpenseBot configuration, auto-approval paused, MoveOps absorbing cost; (3) written confirmation that Deshawn Hartley's delivery in Denver will be completed no later than April 19, 2026, vendor is Swift Relocations, coordinator is Suki Patel.

2 The email body (visible in the send_email or reply_to_email parameters) mentions the damaged prototype in the context of Zara Okoye's relocation (e.g., referencing the Savannah to Atlanta move, or Zara Okoye by name, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✓✗ 67%
✓Run #1The email body references 'Zara in Atlanta' in the context of replacement prototype transport ('When you are ready to ship a replacement prototype to Zara in Atlanta'). While the email doesn't say 'Savannah to Atlanta' explicitly, Zara Okoye is named and Atlanta is mentioned in the prototype damage context.
✓Run #2The email body refers to 'our moving vendor for Zara's relocation' in the prototype damage section, and the original emails confirm this is Zara Okoye's Savannah to Atlanta move. The email explicitly ties the prototype damage to Zara's relocation.
✗Run #3The email body does not mention Zara Okoye, Savannah, or Atlanta in connection with the prototype damage. The agent found Zara Okoye's relocation record in Airtable but did not include her name or the Savannah-to-Atlanta move in the email body.
✓Run #4The email body states: 'when Mosaic is ready to ship a replacement prototype to Zara in Atlanta, MoveOps will coordinate and fully fund that transport' — referencing Zara and Atlanta in connection with the damaged prototype.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly mentions Zara: 'a gap that Zara observed when she compared the packing on Unit #1 versus Unit #2.' The move context (Savannah to Atlanta) is referenced in the email thread being replied to, and Zara is named in the email body.
✗Run #6The email body does not mention Zara Okoye by name, nor reference the Savannah-to-Atlanta relocation. The prototype damage is discussed generically (as 'Prototype Unit #1') without tying it to the specific Zara Okoye move context.
3 The email body identifies Heartland Movers as the moving vendor responsible for the transport during which the prototype was damaged.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly names Heartland Movers as the vendor: 'MoveOps is actively pursuing recovery of the $40,000 gap from Heartland Movers' and references 'unverified crating and the unscheduled Macon depot stop' — tying them to the damage.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly states 'our moving vendor for Zara's relocation, Heartland Movers, carries carrier liability insurance that covers up to $50,000 per item,' clearly naming Heartland Movers as the responsible vendor.
✓Run #3The email body explicitly states 'We are actively pursuing Heartland Movers to accept liability' and references 'Heartland made an unscheduled overnight stop at their Macon, GA depot' — clearly identifying Heartland Movers as the responsible vendor.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly states: 'We are pursuing Heartland Movers for the remaining $40,000 based on the unverified crating, the unscheduled depot stop, and the chain-of-custody failures.'
✓Run #5The email body clearly states: 'We are actively pursuing Heartland Movers to accept liability for the full $90,000' and references 'the crating foam around Unit #1 may not have met the 4-inch minimum density specification from the work order' as a reason to hold Heartland liable.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly states: 'We are actively pursuing Heartland Movers for full liability' in connection with the prototype damage, clearly identifying Heartland Movers as the responsible vendor.
4 The email body mentions the approximately $40,000 insurance coverage gap — specifically that MoveOps' vendor liability policy covers up to $50,000 per item while the prototype is valued at $90,000.
outcome
